summary: Well-scoped caching implementation for fills and market info that reduces REST churn. Comprehensive test coverage (90/90 pass), clean types, and proper cache invalidation on account/env switch.

comments.body: When the cache is warm, `peekCachedMarketInfos` returns data on line 108 and `setMarketInfos(cached)` is called. Then `fetchMarketInfos` on line 115 resolves immediately from the same cache and calls `setMarketInfos(infos)` again with the same reference. React will bail out on the redundant set, so it's harmless â€” but you could short-circuit with an early return after the `if (cached)` branch (similar to what `usePerpsMarketFills` does on line 141-145) to avoid the extra microtask.

The provider imports `clearPerpsMarketFillsModuleCache` and `clearPerpsMarketInfoModuleCache` from hooks. This creates a providerâ†’hook dependency direction. Consider extracting the cache-clear functions into a shared module (e.g. `ui/hooks/perps/cache-utils.ts`) if the hook files grow, to keep the dependency graph clean.
